In {{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|WD=Q2644}} (Shōwa 43), because the colloquial name “Kanbara Festival” became famous, the shrine name was changed from “Gosha Shrine” to “Kanbara Shrine.” === Precincts === * Kotodama Keyaki === Main buildings === * Main sanctuary * Kagura hall * Shrine office === Cultural properties === * Two wooden seated Buddhist-style statues of Hatakeyama Shigemune and wife ** Designated Niigata Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property (sculpture) on March 24, 2009. === Annual events === * {{ill|lt=Kanbara Festival|Q11621265|WD=Q11621265}} * Oracle rite (otakusen) * Plum festival * Chrysanthemum festival === Local information === ; Address * Nagamine-chō 3-18, Chūō Ward, Niigata City, Niigata Prefecture ; Access * Rail ** About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Niigata Station|Q701753|WD=Q701753}} (Bandai exit) * Car ** About 20 minutes from Niigata-Nishi IC or Niigata-Chūō IC via Route 7 bypass === Footnotes === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * {{cite web|ja|url=https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/14668 |title=Kanbara Shrine |publisher=Niigata Tourism Navi |accessdate=2025-02-27 }} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Takakichi Yamashita|title=Revised History of Kanbara Shrine|publisher=Kanbara Shrine|date=1993 }} === External links === * [https://kanbarajinja.jp/ Kanbara Shrine] - Official site {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんはらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kanbara Shrine (Q63148107)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kanbara Shrine (Q63148107)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kanbara Shrine (Q63148107)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:新潟県の旧郷社]] [[Category:越後国の式内社]] [[Category:新潟市中央区の建築物]] [[Category:新潟市中央区の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QKanbara Shrine (Q63148107)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKanbara Shrine (Q63148107)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kanbara Shrine (Q63148107)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Ward|1=Chūō-ku, Niigata}} Nagamine-chō 3-18 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Shiinezu-hiko|Q11120574|WD=Q11120574|lt=Shiinezu-hiko-no-Mikoto|1=Saonetsuhiko}} |神体 = Wooden seated statues of Hatakeyama Shigemune and wife (trad.) |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = Gosha Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = From night of June 30 to July 3 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Kanbara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (かんばらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Nagamine-chō, Chūō Ward, Niigata City, Niigata Prefecture. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto|1=Kukunochi}} * {{ill|lt=Haniyasu|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto|1=Mizuhanome}} * {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}} * Homusukon-no-Mikoto or * {{ill|lt=Shiinezu-hiko|Q11120574|WD=Q11120574|lt=Shiinezu-hiko-no-Mikoto|1=Saonetsuhiko}} === Associated deities === * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} * {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}} * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama Shigetada|Q3128268|WD=Q3128268|1=Hatakeyama Shigetada}} * {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama Shigemune|Q61055753|WD=Q61055753|1=Hatakeyama Shigemune}} === History === The original shrine was “Ōmi no Yashiro,” said to have stood on Kinbachi-yama, with Shiinezu-hiko as principal deity, alongside five-element deities (fire, water, wood, metal, earth), and others including Sugawara no Michizane. Tradition says samurai {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama Shigemune|Q61055753|WD=Q61055753|1=Hatakeyama Shigemune}} of early Kamakura period is enshrined here, so it is also called “Rokurō-sama.” In {{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|WD=Q7671|1=1688}} (Genroku 1), a breach of the {{ill|lt=Shinano River|Q734455|WD=Q734455|1=Shinano River}} led to relocation from Kinbachi-yama to current site. In {{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|WD=Q2644|1=1968}} (Shōwa 43), because the colloquial name “Kanbara Festival” became famous, the shrine name was changed from “Gosha Shrine” to “Kanbara Shrine.” === Precincts === * Kotodama Keyaki === Main buildings === * Main sanctuary * Kagura hall * Shrine office === Cultural properties === * Two wooden seated Buddhist-style statues of Hatakeyama Shigemune and wife ** Designated Niigata Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property (sculpture) on March 24, 2009. === Annual events === * {{ill|lt=Kanbara Festival|Q11621265|WD=Q11621265|1=Kanbara Matsuri}} * Oracle rite (otakusen) * Plum festival * Chrysanthemum festival === Local information === ; Address * Nagamine-chō 3-18, Chūō Ward, Niigata City, Niigata Prefecture ; Access * Rail ** About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Niigata Station|Q701753|WD=Q701753|1=Niigata Station}} (Bandai exit) * Car ** About 20 minutes from Niigata-Nishi IC or Niigata-Chūō IC via Route 7 bypass === Footnotes === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * {{cite web|ja|url=https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/14668 |title=Kanbara Shrine |publisher=Niigata Tourism Navi |accessdate=2025-02-27 }} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Takakichi Yamashita|title=Revised History of Kanbara Shrine|publisher=Kanbara Shrine|date=1993 }} === External links === * [https://kanbarajinja.jp/ Kanbara Shrine] - Official site {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんはらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kanbara Shrine (Q63148107)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kanbara Shrine (Q63148107)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kanbara Shrine (Q63148107)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:新潟県の旧郷社]] [[Category:越後国の式内社]] [[Category:新潟市中央区の建築物]] [[Category:新潟市中央区の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QKanbara Shrine (Q63148107)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKanbara Shrine (Q63148107)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kanbara Shrine (Q63148107)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Gosha Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = From night of June 30 to July 3 |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Niigata|label=Kanbara Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (かんばらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Nagamine-chō, Chūō Ward, Niigata City, Niigata Prefecture. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto|1=Kukunochi}} * {{ill|lt=Haniyasu|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto|1=Mizuhanome}} * {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}} * Homusukon-no-Mikoto or * {{ill|lt=Shiinezu-hiko|Q11120574|WD=Q11120574|lt=Shiinezu-hiko-no-Mikoto|1=Saonetsuhiko}} === Associated deities === * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} * {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}} * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama Shigetada|Q3128268|WD=Q3128268|1=Hatakeyama Shigetada}} * {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama Shigemune|Q61055753|WD=Q61055753|1=Hatakeyama Shigemune}} === History === The original shrine was “Ōmi no Yashiro,” said to have stood on Kinbachi-yama, with Shiinezu-hiko as principal deity, alongside five-element deities (fire, water, wood, metal, earth), and others including Sugawara no Michizane. Tradition says samurai {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama Shigemune|Q61055753|WD=Q61055753|1=Hatakeyama Shigemune}} of early Kamakura period is enshrined here, so it is also called “Rokurō-sama.” In {{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|WD=Q7671|1=1688}} (Genroku 1), a breach of the {{ill|lt=Shinano River|Q734455|WD=Q734455|1=Shinano River}} led to relocation from Kinbachi-yama to current site. In {{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|WD=Q2644|1=1968}} (Shōwa 43), because the colloquial name “Kanbara Festival” became famous, the shrine name was changed from “Gosha Shrine” to “Kanbara Shrine.” === Precincts === * Kotodama Keyaki === Main buildings === * Main sanctuary * Kagura hall * Shrine office === Cultural properties === * Two wooden seated Buddhist-style statues of Hatakeyama Shigemune and wife ** Designated Niigata Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property (sculpture) on March 24, 2009. === Annual events === * {{ill|lt=Kanbara Festival|Q11621265|WD=Q11621265|1=Kanbara Matsuri}} * Oracle rite (otakusen) * Plum festival * Chrysanthemum festival === Local information === ; Address * Nagamine-chō 3-18, Chūō Ward, Niigata City, Niigata Prefecture ; Access * Rail ** About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Niigata Station|Q701753|WD=Q701753|1=Niigata Station}} (Bandai exit) * Car ** About 20 minutes from Niigata-Nishi IC or Niigata-Chūō IC via Route 7 bypass === Footnotes === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * {{cite web|ja|url=https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/14668 |title=Kanbara Shrine |publisher=Niigata Tourism Navi |accessdate=2025-02-27 }} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Takakichi Yamashita|title=Revised History of Kanbara Shrine|publisher=Kanbara Shrine|date=1993 }} === External links === * [https://kanbarajinja.jp/ Kanbara Shrine] - Official site {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:かんはらしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kanbara Shrine (Q63148107)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kanbara Shrine (Q63148107)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kanbara Shrine (Q63148107)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:新潟県の旧郷社]] [[Category:越後国の式内社]] [[Category:新潟市中央区の建築物]] [[Category:新潟市中央区の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QKanbara Shrine (Q63148107)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QKanbara Shrine (Q63148107)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Kanbara Shrine (Q63148107)}}[[Category:qq]]

vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kanbara Shrine''' (かんばらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Nagamine-chō, Chūō Ward, Niigata City, Niigata Prefecture. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto|1=Kukunochi}} * {{ill|lt=Haniyasu|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto|1=Mizuhanome}} * {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}} * Homusukon-no-Mikoto or * {{ill|lt=Shiinezu-hiko|Q11120574|WD=Q11120574|lt=Shiinezu-hiko-no-Mikoto|1=Saonetsuhiko}} === Associated deities === * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} * {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}} * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama Shigetada|Q3128268|WD=Q3128268|1=Hatakeyama Shigetada}} * {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama Shigemune|Q61055753|WD=Q61055753|1=Hatakeyama Shigemune}} === History === The original shrine was “Ōmi no Yashiro,” said to have stood on Kinbachi-yama, with Shiinezu-hiko as principal deity, alongside five-element deities (fire, water, wood, metal, earth), and others including Sugawara no Michizane. Tradition says samurai {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama Shigemune|Q61055753|WD=Q61055753|1=Hatakeyama Shigemune}} of early Kamakura period is enshrined here, so it is also called “Rokurō-sama.” In {{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|WD=Q7671|1=1688}} (Genroku 1), a breach of the {{ill|lt=Shinano River|Q734455|WD=Q734455|1=Shinano River}} led to relocation from Kinbachi-yama to current site. In {{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|WD=Q2644|1=1968}} (Shōwa 43), because the colloquial name “Kanbara Festival” became famous, the shrine name was changed from “Gosha Shrine” to “Kanbara Shrine.” === Precincts === * Kotodama Keyaki === Main buildings === * Main sanctuary * Kagura hall * Shrine office === Cultural properties === * Two wooden seated Buddhist-style statues of Hatakeyama Shigemune and wife ** Designated Niigata Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property (sculpture) on March 24, 2009. === Annual events === * {{ill|lt=Kanbara Festival|Q11621265|WD=Q11621265|1=Kanbara Matsuri}} * Oracle rite (otakusen) * Plum festival * Chrysanthemum festival === Local information === ; Address * Nagamine-chō 3-18, Chūō Ward, Niigata City, Niigata Prefecture ; Access * Rail ** About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Niigata Station|Q701753|WD=Q701753|1=Niigata Station}} (Bandai exit) * Car ** About 20 minutes from Niigata-Nishi IC or Niigata-Chūō IC via Route 7 bypass === Footnotes === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * {{cite web|ja|url=https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/14668 |title=Kanbara Shrine |publisher=Niigata Tourism Navi |accessdate=2025-02-27 }} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Takakichi Yamashita|title=Revised History of Kanbara Shrine|publisher=Kanbara Shrine|date=1993 }} === External links === * [https://kanbarajinja.jp/ Kanbara Shrine] - Official site {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kanbara Shrine (Q63148107)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kanbara Shrine (Q63148107)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kanbara Shrine (Q63148107)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:新潟県の旧郷社]] [[Category:越後国の式内社]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Niigata]] [[Category:新潟市中央区の歴史]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qq]]

Shrine''' (かんばらじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Nagamine-chō, Chūō Ward, Niigata City, Niigata Prefecture. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto|1=Kukunochi}} * {{ill|lt=Haniyasu|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto|1=Mizuhanome}} * {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}} * Homusukon-no-Mikoto or * {{ill|lt=Shiinezu-hiko|Q11120574|WD=Q11120574|lt=Shiinezu-hiko-no-Mikoto|1=Saonetsuhiko}} === Associated deities === * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} * {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}} * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama Shigetada|Q3128268|WD=Q3128268|1=Hatakeyama Shigetada}} * {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama Shigemune|Q61055753|WD=Q61055753|1=Hatakeyama Shigemune}} === History === The original shrine was “Ōmi no Yashiro,” said to have stood on Kinbachi-yama, with Shiinezu-hiko as principal deity, alongside five-element deities (fire, water, wood, metal, earth), and others including Sugawara no Michizane. Tradition says samurai {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama Shigemune|Q61055753|WD=Q61055753|1=Hatakeyama Shigemune}} of early Kamakura period is enshrined here, so it is also called “Rokurō-sama.” In {{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|WD=Q7671|1=1688}} (Genroku 1), a breach of the {{ill|lt=Shinano River|Q734455|WD=Q734455|1=Shinano River}} led to relocation from Kinbachi-yama to current site. In {{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|WD=Q2644|1=1968}} (Shōwa 43), because the colloquial name “Kanbara Festival” became famous, the shrine name was changed from “Gosha Shrine” to “Kanbara Shrine.” === Precincts === * Kotodama Keyaki === Main buildings === * Main sanctuary * Kagura hall * Shrine office === Cultural properties === * Two wooden seated Buddhist-style statues of Hatakeyama Shigemune and wife ** Designated Niigata Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property (sculpture) on March 24, 2009. === Annual events === * {{ill|lt=Kanbara Festival|Q11621265|WD=Q11621265|1=Kanbara Matsuri}} * Oracle rite (otakusen) * Plum festival * Chrysanthemum festival === Local information === ; Address * Nagamine-chō 3-18, Chūō Ward, Niigata City, Niigata Prefecture ; Access * Rail ** About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Niigata Station|Q701753|WD=Q701753|1=Niigata Station}} (Bandai exit) * Car ** About 20 minutes from Niigata-Nishi IC or Niigata-Chūō IC via Route 7 bypass === Footnotes === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * {{cite web|ja|url=https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/14668 |title=Kanbara Shrine |publisher=Niigata Tourism Navi |accessdate=2025-02-27 }} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Takakichi Yamashita|title=Revised History of Kanbara Shrine|publisher=Kanbara Shrine|date=1993 }} === External links === * [https://kanbarajinja.jp/ Kanbara Shrine] - Official site {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kanbara Shrine (Q63148107)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kanbara Shrine (Q63148107)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kanbara Shrine (Q63148107)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:新潟県の神社]] [[Category:新潟県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Niigata]] [[Category:History of Chuo Ward, Niigata City]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qq]]

{{ill|lt=Kukunochi|Q10749920|WD=Q10749920|lt=Kukunochi-no-Mikoto|1=Kukunochi}} * {{ill|lt=Haniyasu|Q9285919|WD=Q9285919|lt=Haniyamahime-no-Mikoto|1=Haniyasu-hiko and Haniyasu-hime}} * {{ill|lt=Mizuhanome|Q8191796|WD=Q8191796|lt=Mizuhanome-no-Mikoto|1=Mizuhanome}} * {{ill|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Kami|Q4387856|WD=Q4387856|lt=Kanayamahiko-no-Mikoto|1=Kanayama-hiko and Kanayama-hime}} * Homusukon-no-Mikoto or * {{ill|lt=Shiinezu-hiko|Q11120574|WD=Q11120574|lt=Shiinezu-hiko-no-Mikoto|1=Saonetsuhiko}} === Associated deities === * {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} * {{ill|lt=Yamato Takeru|Q461258|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru-no-Mikoto|1=Yamato Takeru}} * {{ill|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata|Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata}} * {{ill|lt=Sugawara no Michizane|Q382005|WD=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} * {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama Shigetada|Q3128268|WD=Q3128268|1=Hatakeyama Shigetada}} * {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama Shigemune|Q61055753|WD=Q61055753|1=Hatakeyama Shigemune}} === History === The original shrine was “Ōmi no Yashiro,” said to have stood on Kinbachi-yama, with Shiinezu-hiko as principal deity, alongside five-element deities (fire, water, wood, metal, earth), and others including Sugawara no Michizane. Tradition says samurai {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama Shigemune|Q61055753|WD=Q61055753|1=Hatakeyama Shigemune}} of early Kamakura period is enshrined here, so it is also called “Rokurō-sama.” In {{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|WD=Q7671|1=1688}} (Genroku 1), a breach of the {{ill|lt=Shinano River|Q734455|WD=Q734455|1=Shinano River}} led to relocation from Kinbachi-yama to current site. In {{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|WD=Q2644|1=1968}} (Shōwa 43), because the colloquial name “Kanbara Festival” became famous, the shrine name was changed from “Gosha Shrine” to “Kanbara Shrine.” === Precincts === * Kotodama Keyaki === Main buildings === * Main sanctuary * Kagura hall * Shrine office === Cultural properties === * Two wooden seated Buddhist-style statues of Hatakeyama Shigemune and wife ** Designated Niigata Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property (sculpture) on March 24, 2009. === Annual events === * {{ill|lt=Kanbara Festival|Q11621265|WD=Q11621265|1=Kanbara Matsuri}} * Oracle rite (otakusen) * Plum festival * Chrysanthemum festival === Local information === ; Address * Nagamine-chō 3-18, Chūō Ward, Niigata City, Niigata Prefecture ; Access * Rail ** About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Niigata Station|Q701753|WD=Q701753|1=Niigata Station}} (Bandai exit) * Car ** About 20 minutes from Niigata-Nishi IC or Niigata-Chūō IC via Route 7 bypass === Footnotes === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * {{cite web|ja|url=https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/14668 |title=Kanbara Shrine |publisher=Niigata Tourism Navi |accessdate=2025-02-27 }} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Takakichi Yamashita|title=Revised History of Kanbara Shrine|publisher=Kanbara Shrine|date=1993 }} === External links === * [https://kanbarajinja.jp/ Kanbara Shrine] - Official site {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kanbara Shrine (Q63148107)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kanbara Shrine (Q63148107)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Kanbara Shrine (Q63148107)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:新潟県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Buildings in Chuo-ku, Niigata]] [[Category:History of Chuo Ward, Niigata City]] [[Category:pages]]

Tradition says samurai {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama Shigemune|Q61055753|WD=Q61055753|1=Hatakeyama Shigemune}} of early Kamakura period is enshrined here, so it is also called “Rokurō-sama.” In {{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|WD=Q7671|1=1688}} (Genroku 1), a breach of the {{ill|lt=Shinano River|Q734455|WD=Q734455|1=Shinano River}} led to relocation from Kinbachi-yama to current site. In {{ill|lt=1968|Q2644|WD=Q2644|1=1968}} (Shōwa 43), because the colloquial name “Kanbara Festival” became famous, the shrine name was changed from “Gosha Shrine” to “Kanbara Shrine.” === Precincts === * Kotodama Keyaki === Main buildings === * Main sanctuary * Kagura hall * Shrine office === Cultural properties === * Two wooden seated Buddhist-style statues of Hatakeyama Shigemune and wife ** Designated Niigata Prefecture Tangible Cultural Property (sculpture) on March 24, 2009. === Annual events === * {{ill|lt=Kanbara Festival|Q11621265|WD=Q11621265|1=Kanbara Matsuri}} * Oracle rite (otakusen) * Plum festival * Chrysanthemum festival === Local information === ; Address * Nagamine-chō 3-18, Chūō Ward, Niigata City, Niigata Prefecture ; Access * Rail ** About 15 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Niigata Station|Q701753|WD=Q701753|1=Niigata Station}} (Bandai exit) * Car ** About 20 minutes from Niigata-Nishi IC or Niigata-Chūō IC via Route 7 bypass === Footnotes === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * {{cite web|ja|url=https://niigata-kankou.or.jp/spot/14668 |title=Kanbara Shrine |publisher=Niigata Tourism Navi |accessdate=2025-02-27 }} * {{Cite book|ja|author=Takakichi Yamashita|title=Revised History of Kanbara Shrine|publisher=Kanbara Shrine|date=1993 }} === External links === * [https://kanbarajinja.jp/ Kanbara Shrine] - Official site {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kanbara Shrine (Q63148107)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kanbara Shrine (Q63148107)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kanbara Shrine (Q63148107)]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Niigata Prefecture]] [[Category:新潟県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Echigo Province]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chuo-ku, Niigata]] [[Category:History of Chuo Ward, Niigata City]]

Tradition says samurai {{ill|lt=Hatakeyama Shigemune|Q61055753|qid=Q61055753|1=Hatakeyama Shigemune}} of early Kamakura period is enshrined here, so it is also called “Rokurō-sama.” In {{ill|lt=1688|Q7671|qid=Q7671|1=1688}} (Genroku 1), a breach of the {{ill|lt=Shinano River|Q734455|qid=Q734455|1=Shinano River}} led to relocation from Kinbachi-yama to current site.
